zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Seagrave於1270年7月20日出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾澤亞格拉福時，她的父親Nicholas Segrave 1st Baron Segrave31歲，母親Matilda de Lucy31歲。她於1287年與Sir Alan la Zouche在英國萊希斯特斯爾阿什比 馬尼亞結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1295年11月12日在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾阿什比 德 拉 祖享年25歲，埋葬在英國萊希斯特斯爾阿什比 德 拉 祖。

nameMeanings.name: Seagrave
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Seagrave (Leicestershire), probably from Old English sēath ‘pit, hole’ + grāf ‘grove’.

variants.name: Seagraves
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of Seagrave , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Seagrave was born on 20 July 1270, in Seagrave, Leicestershire, England, her father, Nicholas Segrave 1st Baron Segrave, was 31 and her mother, Matilda de Lucy, was 31. She married Sir Alan la Zouche in 1287, in Ashby Magna, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 12 November 1295, in Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 25, and was buried in Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor de Seagrave est née le 20 juillet 1270, à Seagrave, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Segrave 1st Baron Segrave, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Matilda de Lucy, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Alan la Zouche en 1287, à Ashby Magna, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 novembre 1295, à Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 25 ans, et a été enterrée à Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Seagraveが1270年7月20日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアセアグラブで生まれた時，父Nicholas Segrave 1st Baron Segraveは31歳，母Matilda de Lucyは31歳であった。1287年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアアシュビー マグナでSir Alan la Zoucheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1295年11月12日にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアアシュビー ド ラ ゾウチで25歳で死去し，イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアアシュビー ド ラ ゾウチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Seagrave(이)가 1270년 7월 20일 영국 레이스스터실르 시그레이브에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Segrave 1st Baron Segrave(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda de Lucy(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1287년 영국 레이스스터실르 애쉬비 매그나에서 Sir Alan la Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1295년 11월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 애쉬비 데 라 즈우치에서 25세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 레이스스터실르 애쉬비 데 라 즈우치에 묻혔다.

pt.20 July 1270–12 November 1295: 20 de julho de 1270–12 de novembro de 1295
pt.Barton Seagrave, Northamptonshire, Engla: Barton Seagrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Rockingham Castle, Ashby, Leicestershire: Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom

pt.Ashby-de-la-Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Seagrave nasceu em 20 de julho de 1270, em Seagrave, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Segrave 1st Baron Segrave, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Matilda de Lucy, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Alan la Zouche em 1287, em Ashby Magna, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de novembro de 1295, em Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 25 anos, e foi sepultada em Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.Rockingham Castle, Ashby, Leicestershire: ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия
ru.Ashby-de-la-Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Seagrave родилась 20 июля 1270 г. в местности сиграв, лисестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Segrave 1st Baron Segrave было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Matilda de Lucy, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Alan la Zouche в 1287 г. в местности ашби магна, лисестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 12 ноября 1295 г. в местности ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 25 лет и была похоронена в местности ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor de Seagrave nació el 20 de julio de 1270, en Seagrave, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Segrave 1st Baron Segrave, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Matilda de Lucy, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Sir Alan la Zouche en 1287, en Ashby Magna, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 12 de noviembre de 1295, en Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 25 años, y fue sepultada en Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.
